title: Arthur the King Box Office Underperformance Analysis - Deadline
text: Mark Wahlberg's dog adventure film Arthur the King opened to $7.5 million, falling short of the projected $8M-$10M range despite receiving strong audience ratings. Several key factors contributed to the underperformance. The film looked ripe for streaming by today's standards and not theatrical, potentially conditioning audiences to stay home. Lionsgate targeted only male sports demographics, neglecting family and faith-based audiences who traditionally support dog movies. The studio chose not to emphasize family appeal because the Belgian malinois wasn't considered cute enough. Wahlberg's recent Apple TV+ film The Family Plan was reportedly the streamer's most-watched movie ever. As one source notes, if they see you for free all week, they won't pay to see you on the weekend. The actual production cost was approximately $40 million—too expensive for this concept, which featured low-stakes adventure racing similar to the TV show Amazing Race. Spring break wasn't in full force, particularly in the Northeast, impacting family attendance. The film was sold exclusively to Prime Video in Canada, removing that revenue source from theatrical. These combined factors resulted in a disappointing opening despite positive audience reception with an A CinemaScore.
